title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: Rfmalloc R package providing file-backed ALTREP vector types powered bundled fmalloc allocator runtime. package supports persistent scratch runtimes, ALTREP serialization, explicit native/R destroy path via destroy_fmalloc_vector().

title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: making package changes, load package-development guidance r-package-development: /root/.pi/agent/git/github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/pi-skills/skills/r-package-development/SKILL.md. Follow repo’s existing conventions R/, src/, man/, inst/tinytest/.

title: AGENTS for Rfmalloc
text: Keep newest entries top ## Development (unreleased) (latest section). Add concise, user-visible bullets grouped feature/fix area. Prefer one bullet per behavior change (e.g. persistence/unserialize semantics, destroy/recovery behavior, diagnostics). edit historical release notes; append new work top section.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc experimental R package file-backed R vectors using patched copy fmalloc. current R-facing vector API creates fmalloc-backed ALTREP vectors atomic, character, list storage. Earlier prototypes explored R’s custom allocator / Rf_allocVector3() path (see Simon Urbanek’s proof--concept). package moved direct ALTREP vectors public vector API control copy--write duplication. patched fmalloc malloc/free/realloc layer still built installed native consumers use C API link bundled library.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Implemented now: fmalloc-backed ALTREP vectors logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list values; list elements restricted NULL Rfmalloc vectors runtime; explicit runtime handles persistent / scratch runtime modes; persistent serialization reopening fixed-width atomic character vectors; fmalloc-backed coercion subset-copy results supported vector types; nested list-container recovery reference persistent runtimes; explicit destroy_fmalloc_vector() immediate vector invalidation/cleanup parent-reference safety checks; -file persistent allocation catalog used validate serialized references; installed C header R_RegisterCCallable() API packages. Still experimental: view-based subsets (currently copy-; true views form additional semantic layer), richer catalog maintenance tooling, mapped-file recovery diagnostics.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc")

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: examples use temporary backing files. Several chunks wrap code local() .exit() can clean files README rendered; required normal interactive use. code, keep runtime handle need call cleanup_fmalloc() finished. shortest form uses init_fmalloc(), opens backing file stores package default runtime. Calls create_fmalloc_vector() can omit runtime argument: multiple backing files explicit lifetime management, prefer runtime handles returned open_fmalloc():
code: library(Rfmalloc) local({   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   init_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc()     unlink(alloc_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10)   v_num <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10)   v_raw <- create_fmalloc_vector("raw", 4)   v_cplx <- create_fmalloc_vector("complex", 4)   v_chr <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3)   v_lst <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2)    v_int[1:3] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   v_num[1:3] <- c(1.1, 2.2, 3.3)   v_raw[] <- as.raw(1:4)   v_cplx[] <- c(1+1i, 2+2i, 3+3i, 4+4i)   v_chr[] <- c("alpha", "beta", NA_character_)    list_child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2)   list_child[] <- 1:2   v_lst[[1]] <- list_child    list(     integer = v_int[1:3],     numeric = v_num[1:3],     raw = v_raw[],     complex = v_cplx[],     character = v_chr[],     list_first = v_lst[[1]][]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5062f2c4c7.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $integer #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> $numeric #> [1] 1.1 2.2 3.3 #>  #> $raw #> [1] 01 02 03 04 #>  #> $complex #> [1] 1+1i 2+2i 3+3i 4+4i #>  #> $character #> [1] "alpha" "beta"  NA      #>  #> $list_first #> [1] 1 2 local({   handle_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(handle_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(handle_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt)   v[1:3] <- 10:12   v[1:3] }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507c2aa3d6.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 10 11 12

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
code: local({   large_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   init_fmalloc(large_file, size_gb = 5)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc()     unlink(large_file)   }, add = TRUE)    big_int <- local({     x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 1e9) # about 4 GB of payload     x[1:5] <- 1:5     x   })   big_int[1:5] }) #> Requested file size: 5.00 GB (5368709120 bytes) #> Creating file with size: 5368709120 bytes (5.00 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505f11392c.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=1000000000 #> Large allocation: 3814.70 MB requested #> SUCCESS: fmalloc allocated 4000000000 bytes #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: destroy_fmalloc_vector() immediately releases native bookkeeping one fmalloc ALTREP vector returns logical indicating whether live vector destroyed. helper enforces parent-reference safety: vector destroyed still stored child fmalloc list. needed, drop parent links first. persistent mode, destroying default semantics retains payload bytes recorded -disk allocation remains recoverable normal serialize() flows. Use destroy_fmalloc_vector(x, unsafe = TRUE) reclaim persistent payload memory mark catalog entry non-recoverable. scoped targeted vector(s): one object unsafe-destroyed, objects runtime remain recoverable.
code: local({   destroy_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(destroy_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(destroy_file)   }, add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    parent <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   parent[[1]] <- child    destroy_error <- try(destroy_fmalloc_vector(child), silent = TRUE)    parent[[1]] <- NULL   destroy_ok <- destroy_fmalloc_vector(child)    list(     destroy_rejected_with_parent = inherits(destroy_error, "try-error"),     destroy_succeeded_after_unset = destroy_ok   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5027a6fdf6.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $destroy_rejected_with_parent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $destroy_succeeded_after_unset #> [1] TRUE local({   selective_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(selective_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(selective_file)   }, add = TRUE)    keep <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   drop <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   keep[] <- c(101L, 202L, 303L)   drop[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    keep_blob <- serialize(keep, NULL)   drop_blob <- serialize(drop, NULL)    destroy_fmalloc_vector(drop, unsafe = TRUE)    keep_recovered <- unserialize(keep_blob)   drop_recover_error <- try(unserialize(drop_blob), silent = TRUE)    list(     keep_recovered_ok = all.equal(keep_recovered[], c(101L, 202L, 303L)) == TRUE,     drop_recover_fails = inherits(drop_recover_error, "try-error")   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503c648d94.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503c648d94.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503c648d94.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503c648d94.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503c648d94.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $keep_recovered_ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $drop_recover_fails #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: open_fmalloc() init_fmalloc() accept mode = "persistent" mode = "scratch". default "persistent". persistent mode, committed vector payloads returned fmalloc vector finalizers. Fixed-width atomic vectors serialize reference physical allocation backing file. scratch mode, backing file temporary allocation arena. Vector finalizers may return payloads fmalloc, serialization falls back ordinary R values.
code: local({   persist_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(persist_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(persist_file)   }, add = TRUE)    x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 5, runtime = rt)   x[] <- 1:5   roundtrip <- unserialize(serialize(x, NULL))   roundtrip[] }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505597efe7.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505597efe7.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505597efe7.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: R’s internal inspector dispatches ALTREP Inspect hook. Rfmalloc’s inspector reports vector type, length, payload byte count, runtime mode, runtime state, payload offset, file UUID, backing-file path. inspect() output internal R diagnostic, exact formatting can vary R versions. fmalloc_altrep ... line comes Rfmalloc’s ALTREP Inspect method.
code: local({   inspect_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(inspect_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(inspect_file)   }, add = TRUE)    inspect_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   inspect_vec[] <- 1:4   .Internal(inspect(inspect_vec)) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb501af6c5f9.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> @5bc2b4adde20 13 INTSXP g0c0 [REF(1)] fmalloc_altrep type=integer length=4 bytes=16 data=0x75380de023e8 mode=persistent runtime=open offset=9192 uuid=fdb93c9b99687cfed0d689343863ee5e file=/tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb501af6c5f9.bin #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc character vectors ALTSTRING vectors. String bytes, lengths, encodings, NA flags live fmalloc storage. R CHARSXP values materialized STRING_ELT() access; Rfmalloc allocate CHARSXP objects inside fmalloc.
code: local({   char_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(char_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(char_file)   }, add = TRUE)    chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("one", NA_character_, "three")    from_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   from_int[] <- 1:3    list(     chars = chars[],     from_integer = as.character(from_int)[]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5044988e8a.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $chars #> [1] "one"   NA      "three" #>  #> $from_integer #> [1] "1" "2" "3"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Use tracemem() confirm R-style copy--write higher-level wrappers:
code: local({   cow_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(cow_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(cow_file)   }, add = TRUE)    fm_matrix <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 6, runtime = rt)   fm_matrix[] <- 1:6   dim(fm_matrix) <- c(2L, 3L)   dimnames(fm_matrix) <- list(c("r1", "r2"), c("c1", "c2", "c3"))    # Only writes should trigger a copy trace.   tracemem(fm_matrix)   matrix_copy_events_on_read <- capture.output({     fm_matrix_view <- fm_matrix     invisible(fm_matrix_view[1L, 1L])   })    fm_matrix_copy <- fm_matrix   matrix_copy_events_on_write <- capture.output({     fm_matrix_copy[1L, 2L] <- 99L   })    fm_col_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   fm_col_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   fm_col_a[] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   fm_col_b[] <- c(4L, 5L, 6L)   fm_df <- data.frame(a = fm_col_a, b = fm_col_b, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    tracemem(fm_df)   df_copy_events_on_read <- capture.output({     fm_df_view <- fm_df     invisible(fm_df_view$a[1L])   })    fm_df_copy <- fm_df   df_copy_events_on_write <- capture.output({     fm_df_copy$a[1L] <- 99L   })   untracemem(fm_matrix)   untracemem(fm_df)    list(     matrix_copy_on_read = length(matrix_copy_events_on_read),     matrix_copy_on_write = length(matrix_copy_events_on_write),     data_frame_copy_on_read = length(df_copy_events_on_read),     data_frame_copy_on_write = length(df_copy_events_on_write),     matrix_original = fm_matrix[1L, 2L],     matrix_copy = fm_matrix_copy[1L, 2L],     data_frame_original = fm_df$a[],     data_frame_copy = fm_df_copy$a[]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505529e6d5.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $matrix_copy_on_read #> [1] 0 #>  #> $matrix_copy_on_write #> [1] 1 #>  #> $data_frame_copy_on_read #> [1] 0 #>  #> $data_frame_copy_on_write #> [1] 2 #>  #> $matrix_original #> [1] 3 #>  #> $matrix_copy #> [1] 99 #>  #> $data_frame_original #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> $data_frame_copy #> [1] 99  2  3

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Runtime handles make possible use one backing file one R process. Calling cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a) marks rt_a closed. vectors rt_a still reachable, native mapping kept alive vectors garbage-collected. implemented native live-vector count ALTREP-held external pointer fmalloc-backed vector; user-visible attribute added vector.
code: local({   file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(file_a)   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(file_b, size_gb = 0.1)    vec_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt_a)   vec_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10, runtime = rt_b)   vec_a[1] <- 101L   vec_b[1] <- 202    before_cleanup <- data.frame(     vector = c("vec_a", "vec_b"),     value = c(vec_a[1], vec_b[1])   )    cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)   after_cleanup <- vec_a[1]   cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)   unlink(c(file_a, file_b))    list(     before_cleanup = before_cleanup,     vec_a_after_cleanup = after_cleanup   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb50509e4595.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Requested file size: 0.10 GB (107374182 bytes) #> Creating file with size: 107374182 bytes (0.10 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb501c85c2db.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $before_cleanup #>   vector value #> 1  vec_a   101 #> 2  vec_b   202 #>  #> $vec_a_after_cleanup #> [1] 101

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc ALTREP serialization path persistent runtimes. persistent fixed-width atomic character vectors, serialize() stores small reference record instead copying vector payload serialization stream. record contains: format tag: "RfmallocRef"; reference format version; backing-file path; backing-file UUID; R vector type; vector length; payload offset inside mapped file; payload byte size. unserialize(), Rfmalloc reopens backing file, verifies UUID, checks recorded dimensions file bounds, validates catalog record generation, reconstructs ALTREP vector around fmalloc allocation. Scratch runtimes use ordinary R serialization instead. serialized values depend reopening scratch backing file. Rfmalloc list vectors intentionally general R lists. Element assignment accepts NULL Rfmalloc-backed vectors runtime; ordinary R objects base vectors, data frames, arbitrary lists rejected. keeps lists inside file-backed object universe. persistent runtimes, list containers now serialize reference state children recoverable fmalloc vectors. serialized state stores per-slot child descriptors, nested list containers can reopen recursively backing file without carrying session-local SEXP pointers. reference-based recovery, name-based object discovery. catalog stores physical allocation records, user variable names. Serialized references use catalog record offset generation validation; catalog can listed, yet high-level object store recovering vectors name.
code: local({   ser_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ser_file, mode = "persistent")    ints <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   ints[] <- 101:104   chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("alpha", NA_character_, "gamma")    catalog <- list_fmalloc_allocations(rt)   ints_blob <- serialize(ints, NULL)   chars_blob <- serialize(chars, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    ints_recovered <- unserialize(ints_blob)   chars_recovered <- unserialize(chars_blob)   output <- list(     catalog = catalog[, c("record_offset", "generation", "type", "length")],     integers = ints_recovered[],     characters = chars_recovered[]   )    unlink(ser_file)   output }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507f7887a2.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507f7887a2.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507f7887a2.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507f7887a2.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507f7887a2.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> $catalog #>   record_offset generation      type length #> 1          9440          2 character      3 #> 2          9224          1   integer      4 #>  #> $integers #> [1] 101 102 103 104 #>  #> $characters #> [1] "alpha" NA      "gamma" local({   scratch_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   scratch_rt <- open_fmalloc(scratch_file, mode = "scratch")    scratch_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = scratch_rt)   scratch_vec[] <- 1:4   scratch_copy <- unserialize(serialize(scratch_vec, NULL))    cleanup_fmalloc(scratch_rt)   unlink(scratch_file)   scratch_copy }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507ba32b78.bin (init: true, mode: scratch) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> [1] 1 2 3 4

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: List assignment rejects non-fmalloc payloads runtime. Nested fmalloc lists recover recursively serialized unserialized persistent backing file. Cross-runtime insertion also rejected.
code: local({   reject_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(reject_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(reject_file)   }, add = TRUE)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- c(1L, 2L)   fm_list[[1]] <- child    bad_assignment <- try({     fm_list[[2]] <- 1:2   }, silent = TRUE)    list(     same_runtime_child = fm_list[[1]][],     rejected_non_fmalloc_payload = inherits(bad_assignment, "try-error")   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb505ab464a4.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $same_runtime_child #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $rejected_non_fmalloc_payload #> [1] TRUE local({   recover_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(recover_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit(unlink(recover_file), add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    nested <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   nested[[1]] <- child    labels <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   labels[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   fm_list[[1]] <- nested   fm_list[[2]] <- labels    blob <- serialize(fm_list, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    recovered <- unserialize(blob)   list(     recovered_nested = recovered[[1]][[1]][],     recovered_labels = recovered[[2]]   ) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5025b629a.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Using existing file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5025b629a.bin (size: 33562624 bytes) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb5025b629a.bin (init: false, mode: persistent) #> $recovered_nested #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $recovered_labels #> [1] "alpha" "beta" local({   cross_file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   cross_file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_a, mode = "persistent")   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_b, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)     unlink(c(cross_file_a, cross_file_b))   }, add = TRUE)    child_other_runtime <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt_b)   child_other_runtime[] <- c(10L, 20L)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt_a)   cross_runtime_error <- try({     fm_list[[1]] <- child_other_runtime   }, silent = TRUE)    list(cross_runtime_rejected = inherits(cross_runtime_error, "try-error")) }) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503ebff02f.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Creating file with size: 33562624 bytes (0.03 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb507a76c7ec.bin (init: true, mode: persistent) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up #> $cross_runtime_rejected #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc vectors regular R vectors R’s point view, different operations exercise different paths R ALTREP: vectorized sequential operations sum(x) can use contiguous payload access; scalar loops use ALTREP element access call-heavy; x[] creates fmalloc-backed subset copy supported vector types; bench::mark() reports R heap allocation mem_alloc; count bytes stored fmalloc mapped file. small benchmark uses bench scratch runtime, benchmark-fmalloc allocations preserved persistent records. Timings machine- R-version-specific; use template local measurements.
code: perf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin") rt <- open_fmalloc(perf_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.1) #> Requested file size: 0.10 GB (107374182 bytes) #> Creating file with size: 107374182 bytes (0.10 GB) #> fmalloc initialized with file: /tmp/Rtmp3fxpHu/file13eb503a62b24f.bin (init: true, mode: scratch)  n <- 100000L set.seed(1) idx <- sample.int(n, 2000L)  base_int <- integer(n) base_int[] <- seq_len(n)  fm_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=100000 #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector fm_int[] <- base_int  write_env <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) write_env$base <- base_int write_env$fm <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) #> Creating fmalloc ALTREP vector: type=integer, length=100000 #> Successfully created fmalloc ALTREP vector write_env$fm[] <- base_int  scalar_sum <- function(x, i) {   total <- 0L   for (j in i) total <- total + x[[j]]   total }  perf_result <- bench::mark(   base_sequential_sum = sum(base_int),   fmalloc_sequential_sum = sum(fm_int),   base_scalar_read = scalar_sum(base_int, idx),   fmalloc_scalar_read = scalar_sum(fm_int, idx),   base_subset_copy = base_int[idx],   fmalloc_subset_copy = fm_int[idx],   base_indexed_write = {     write_env$base[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$base[1L])   },   fmalloc_indexed_write = {     write_env$fm[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$fm[1L])   },   iterations = 20,   check = FALSE )[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "mem_alloc", "gc/sec")]  rm(fm_int, write_env) invisible(gc()) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) #> Cleaning up fmalloc... #> fmalloc cleaned up unlink(perf_file) perf_result #> # A tibble: 8 × 5 #>   expression               median `itr/sec` mem_alloc `gc/sec` #>   <bch:expr>             <bch:tm>     <dbl> <bch:byt>    <dbl> #> 1 base_sequential_sum     33.59µs    28028.        0B        0 #> 2 fmalloc_sequential_sum  33.61µs    26763.        0B        0 #> 3 base_scalar_read        32.33µs    28451.   24.55KB        0 #> 4 fmalloc_scalar_read     69.46µs    13969.        0B        0 #> 5 base_subset_copy         4.09µs   245653.    7.86KB        0 #> 6 fmalloc_subset_copy      7.52µs   123091.        0B        0 #> 7 base_indexed_write     107.88µs    11475.  390.67KB        0 #> 8 fmalloc_indexed_write     184µs     5648.        0B        0

what: Native C API for Other Packages
title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc installs inst/include/Rfmalloc.h registers C-callable entry points R_RegisterCCallable(). Downstream packages can add Rfmalloc LinkingTo Imports, include header, use inline wrappers. current native surface exposes runtime open/cleanup, vector creation, default-runtime lookup, catalog listing, API-version query. Returned SEXP objects follow normal R API ownership rules.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: fmalloc: https://github.com/yasukata/fmalloc Simon Urbanek’s Rf_allocVector3() custom mmap allocator PoC: https://gist.github.com/s-u/6712c97ca74181f5a1a5 Rfmalloc’s prior custom allocator implementation using Rf_allocVector3() (historical comparison): https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc/commit/0165953

what: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Rfmalloc provides experimental memory-mapped file allocation capabilities R using patched copy fmalloc library. current package exposes ALTREP file-backed vector allocation logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list vectors fmalloc payload storage.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: open_fmalloc Open explicit fmalloc runtime handle. init_fmalloc Open install default fmalloc runtime. create_fmalloc_vector Create vectors using fmalloc. create_fmalloc_matrix Create matrix-shaped fmalloc vectors. create_fmalloc_array Create array-shaped fmalloc vectors. create_fmalloc_data_frame Create data.frames fmalloc-backed columns. as_fmalloc_matrix Convert fmalloc vectors matrix-shaped objects. as_fmalloc_array Convert fmalloc vectors array-shaped objects. as_fmalloc_data_frame Convert fmalloc-backed objects data.frame. cleanup_fmalloc Request cleanup fmalloc runtime.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: ALTREP file-backed allocation logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list vectors. List elements restricted NULL Rfmalloc-backed vectors runtime. Large allocations spanning multiple fmalloc chunks. Multiple runtime handles one R process. Persistent scratch runtime modes. Reference serialization persistent fixed-width atomic character ALTREP vectors. Fmalloc-backed ALTREP subset copies vector indexing operations. -file allocation catalog persistent vectors. C-callable API installed header packages. Native lifetime tracking runtime mappings outlive reachable vectors allocated .

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Future work includes view-based subset representations, catalog compaction reset tooling, metadata storage attributes persisted elements, robust nested-list reference validation, compaction recovery metadata.

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com contributors: Kenichi Yasukata (fmalloc) [copyright holder] Wolfram Gloger (ptmalloc3) [copyright holder] Free Software Foundation, Inc. (selected GNU C Library support files) [copyright holder]

what: Convert a vector to fmalloc array metadata — as_fmalloc_array
title: Convert a vector to fmalloc array metadata — as_fmalloc_array
text: Returns existing vector re-typed array installing array dimensions (optional dimnames) metadata.

title: Convert a vector to fmalloc array metadata — as_fmalloc_array
code: as_fmalloc_array(x, dim = NULL, dimnames = NULL, copy = TRUE)

title: Convert a vector to fmalloc array metadata — as_fmalloc_array
text: x vector. dim Target dimension vector. dimnames Optional dimnames resulting array. copy TRUE (default), allocate new fmalloc-backed array object. FALSE, install metadata place fmalloc ALTREP payload without allocation (also updates aliases x).

title: Convert a vector to fmalloc array metadata — as_fmalloc_array
text: array object, backed payload copy = FALSE.

title: Convert to data.frame for fmalloc vectors — as_fmalloc_data_frame

title: Clean Up fmalloc — cleanup_fmalloc
text: Requests cleanup fmalloc runtime. vectors allocated runtime still reachable, native mapping kept alive vectors garbage-collected.

title: Clean Up fmalloc — cleanup_fmalloc
code: cleanup_fmalloc(runtime = NULL)

title: Clean Up fmalloc — cleanup_fmalloc
text: runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, current default runtime cleaned .

title: Clean Up fmalloc — cleanup_fmalloc
text: NULL (invisibly)

title: Clean Up fmalloc — cleanup_fmalloc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ init_fmalloc("data.bin") v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 100) rm(v) gc() cleanup_fmalloc() } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt) y <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt) x[] <- 1:3 y[] <- c("a", "b", "c") df <- create_fmalloc_data_frame(x = x, y = y) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) } # }

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
text: Creates ALTREP vector using file-backed fmalloc runtime. returned object ALTREP creation time. Fixed-width atomic payload bytes allocated directly fmalloc, ALTREP duplication vector subsetting keep copy--write copies fmalloc-backed without using R's non-API Rf_allocVector3() path.

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
code: create_fmalloc_vector(type = "integer", length, runtime = NULL)

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
text: type Character string specifying vector type. Supported values "logical", "integer", "numeric"/"double", "raw", "complex", "character", "list". Fixed-width atomic types expose direct writable fmalloc DATAPTR; character vectors store string bytes fmalloc materialize R CHARSXP values demand; list vectors use ALTREP element access R-visible reference sidecar GC safety accept NULL fmalloc-backed vectors runtime elements. Persistent list containers serialized nested reference states elements recoverable runtime. length Integer specifying non-negative length vector create. runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, default runtime established init_fmalloc() used.

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
text: vector specified type length, allocated using fmalloc.

title: Create Vector Using fmalloc — create_fmalloc_vector
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 1000, runtime = rt) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) } # }

what: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Releases runtime bookkeeping single fmalloc ALTREP vector immediately. scratch mode, payload memory immediately reclaimed. persistent mode, vector payload retained default existing -disk state remains durable; optional unsafe = TRUE reclaims payload memory marks metadata non-recoverable.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
code: destroy_fmalloc_vector(x, unsafe = FALSE)

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: x Fmalloc ALTREP vector destroy. unsafe Whether physically free persistent payload bytes. Unsafe destroy intended short-lived scratch-like cleanup mark catalog entry non-recoverable.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Logical value indicating whether live vector destroyed.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
text: Explicit destroy fails vector still referenced another fmalloc list vector child.

title: Explicitly destroy a fmalloc vector — destroy_fmalloc_vector
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin"), mode = "persistent") v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt) destroy_fmalloc_vector(v) } # }

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: Compatibility wrapper opens fmalloc runtime installs package default runtime used create_fmalloc_vector() explicit runtime supplied.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
code: init_fmalloc(filepath, size_gb = NULL, mode = c("persistent", "scratch"))

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: filepath Character string specifying file path fmalloc data. size_gb Numeric value specifying size backing file GB (optional). specified, uses package default size new files existing file size. mode Runtime mode. "persistent" keeps committed vector payloads backing file serializes fixed-width atomic vectors reference. "scratch" uses backing file large temporary allocation arena serializes vectors value.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: Logical indicating whether file newly initialized.

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
text: new code, prefer open_fmalloc() pass returned runtime handle create_fmalloc_vector().

title: Initialize fmalloc — init_fmalloc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin") init_fmalloc(alloc_file) v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 1000) cleanup_fmalloc() unlink(alloc_file) } # }

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: Returns -file allocation catalog persistent fmalloc runtime. catalog stored backing file records physical allocation metadata used validate serialized persistent references.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
code: list_fmalloc_allocations(runtime = NULL)

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: runtime Optional runtime handle returned open_fmalloc(). supplied, default runtime established init_fmalloc() used.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
text: data frame one row per catalog record columns describing catalog record offset, generation, state, vector type, length, payload offset, payload byte size, flags, whether record recoverable reference serialization.

title: List Persistent fmalloc Allocations — list_fmalloc_allocations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rt <- open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt) list_fmalloc_allocations(rt) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) } # }

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: Opens file-backed fmalloc runtime returns external-pointer handle. Multiple runtime handles may open one R process. Runtime mode controls whether vector payloads durable persistent allocations scratch allocations can returned fmalloc ALTREP handles garbage-collected.

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
code: open_fmalloc(filepath, size_gb = NULL, mode = c("persistent", "scratch"))

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: filepath Character string specifying file path fmalloc data. size_gb Numeric value specifying size backing file GB (optional). specified, uses package default size new files existing file size. mode Runtime mode. "persistent" keeps committed vector payloads backing file serializes fixed-width atomic vectors reference. "scratch" uses backing file large temporary allocation arena serializes vectors value.

title: Open an fmalloc Runtime — open_fmalloc
text: external pointer class fmalloc_runtime.
